questions.text: What is the opening command given to the Prophet in this Surah?
questions.answer: To fear Allah and not obey the disbelievers and the hypocrites.

questions.text: What does Allah say about a man having 'two hearts' in his interior?
questions.answer: Allah has not made for any man two hearts in his interior, signifying that one cannot hold two contradictory beliefs or loyalties at once.

questions.text: What is the ruling on 'Zihar' (calling one's wife 'mother') mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah has not made your wives whom you declare as your mothers your actual mothers; it is just a word from your mouths.

questions.text: What is the command regarding the naming of adopted children?
questions.answer: Call them by [the names of] their fathers; it is more just in the sight of Allah. If you do not know their fathers, then they are your brothers in religion.

questions.text: How is the Prophet's relationship with the believers described?
questions.answer: The Prophet is closer to the believers than their own selves, and his wives are their mothers.

questions.text: What is the 'Covenant' (Mithaq) Allah took from the prophets including Nuh, Ibrahim, Musa, and Isa?
questions.answer: A solemn covenant to deliver the message and remain faithful to the truth.

questions.text: How did Allah help the believers during the Battle of the Trench (Al-Ahzab)?
questions.answer: He sent against the enemies a wind and hosts (angels) that the people could not see.

questions.text: How is the state of the believers' hearts described during the intense siege of Madinah?
questions.answer: The eyes swerved, the hearts reached the throats, and they were thinking various thoughts about Allah.

questions.text: What did the hypocrites say when they saw the combined armies approaching?
questions.answer: They said, 'Allah and His Messenger did not promise us except delusion.'

questions.text: Why did a group of the hypocrites ask the Prophet for permission to leave the battle?
questions.answer: They claimed their houses were exposed/vulnerable, but Allah says they only intended to flee.

questions.text: Would fleeing benefit someone if death or killing was decreed for them?
questions.answer: No, if they fled, they would not enjoy life except for a little while.

questions.text: Who are the 'hinders' mentioned who discourage others from fighting for Allah's sake?
questions.answer: The hypocrites who say to their brothers, 'Come to us,' and do not join the battle except a little.

questions.text: How do the hypocrites behave when fear comes vs. when fear departs?
questions.answer: When fear comes, they look at the Prophet with eyes rolling like one fainting; when fear departs, they lash out with sharp tongues, greedy for wealth.

questions.text: Who is the 'Excellent Example' (Uswatun Hasanah) for the believers?
questions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW), for those who hope in Allah and the Last Day and remember Allah much.

questions.text: What was the reaction of the true believers when they saw the combined armies (Ahzab)?
questions.answer: They said, 'This is what Allah and His Messenger had promised us, and Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth.' It only increased them in faith and submission.

questions.text: Who are the 'men' who have been true to their covenant with Allah?
questions.answer: The believers who remained steadfast; some have fulfilled their vow (died as martyrs) and some are still waiting.

questions.text: What was the outcome of the Battle of the Trench for the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Allah repelled them in their rage without them achieving any good; Allah was sufficient for the believers in battle.

questions.text: What happened to the People of the Scripture who supported the combined forces?
questions.answer: Allah brought them down from their fortresses and cast terror into their hearts; a party the Muslims killed and a party they took captive.

questions.text: What choice did the Prophet give to his wives regarding worldly life?
questions.answer: To choose between the life of this world and its adornment (and be released kindly) or Allah, His Messenger, and the Home of the Hereafter.

questions.text: How is the punishment or reward of the Prophet's wives different from other women?
questions.answer: Whoever commits a clear immorality would have a double punishment, and whoever is obedient and does good would have a double reward.

questions.text: What title is given to the wives of the Prophet in this Surah?
questions.answer: Mothers of the Believers (Ummahat al-Mu'minin).

questions.text: What social etiquette were the Prophet's wives commanded to follow?
questions.answer: Not to be soft in speech [to men] so that the diseased of heart would desire them, but to speak with appropriate speech.

questions.text: Where were the Prophet's wives commanded to remain as a sign of dignity?
questions.answer: To remain in their houses and not display themselves like the former times of ignorance (Jahiliyyah).

questions.text: What is the 'Ayah of Purification' (Ayat al-Tathir)?
questions.answer: 'Allah intends only to remove from you the impurity [of sin], O people of the [Prophet's] household (Ahl al-Bayt), and to purify you with [thorough] purification.'

questions.text: What were the Prophet's wives told to remember from their homes?
questions.answer: The verses of Allah and the wisdom (the Sunnah) which are recited in their houses.

questions.text: List some of the qualities of the Muslim men and women whom Allah has prepared forgiveness and a great reward.
questions.answer: Submitting, believing, obedient, truthful, patient, humble, charitable, fasting, guarding their chastity, and remembering Allah much.

questions.text: What is the rule when Allah and His Messenger have decided a matter?
questions.answer: It is not for a believing man or woman to have any choice about their affair.

questions.text: Who was the companion mentioned by name in this Surah?
questions.answer: Zayd (bin Harithah) (RA), in the context of his divorce and the Prophet's subsequent marriage.

questions.text: Why did Allah command the Prophet to marry the former wife of his adopted son Zayd?
questions.answer: To establish that there is no blame upon the believers concerning the wives of their adopted sons when they have finished their marriage.

questions.text: What is the 'Seal of the Prophets' (Khatam an-Nabiyyin)?
questions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW); he is the last of the Prophets.

questions.text: What are the four roles of the Prophet mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: As a witness, a bringer of glad tidings, a warner, and one who invites to Allah by His permission, and an illuminating lamp.

questions.text: What is the ruling regarding the 'Iddah' (waiting period) for women divorced before the marriage is consummated?
questions.answer: There is no waiting period for them to count.

questions.text: Which categories of women were made lawful for the Prophet specifically?
questions.answer: Those to whom he gave their bridal gift, those his right hand possessed from war captives, daughters of his paternal/maternal uncles/aunts who emigrated with him, and a believing woman who gives herself to the Prophet.

questions.text: Was the Prophet permitted to marry any more women after the specific ruling in this Surah?
questions.answer: No, it was not lawful for him to marry any more women after that or to replace his wives with others, except what his right hand possessed.

questions.text: What is the etiquette for entering the Prophet's house as a guest?
questions.answer: Do not enter unless invited for a meal, do not wait for its preparation, and when finished, disperse without staying for conversation.

questions.text: How should people ask the wives of the Prophet for anything?
questions.answer: Ask them from behind a partition (Hijab); that is purer for your hearts and their hearts.

questions.text: Was it permissible for anyone to marry the wives of the Prophet after him?
questions.answer: No, it was never permissible, for it would be an enormity in the sight of Allah.

questions.text: What is the command to the believers regarding sending blessings on the Prophet?
questions.answer: Allah and His angels send blessings upon the Prophet, so believers should ask Allah to confer blessings and peace upon him.

questions.text: What is the consequence of hurting or insulting Allah and His Messenger?
questions.answer: Allah has cursed them in this world and the Hereafter and prepared for them a humiliating punishment.

questions.text: What is the command regarding 'Hijab' for the Prophet's wives, daughters, and all believing women?
questions.answer: To bring down over themselves part of their outer garments (Jalabib) so they will be known and not abused.

questions.text: Who are the 'Murjifun' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Those who spread rumors and false news in Madinah to cause panic and division.

questions.text: What will happen to the hypocrites and rumor-mongers if they do not desist?
questions.answer: Allah will incite the Prophet against them, and they will not remain his neighbors except for a little while, being cursed wherever they are found.

questions.text: Does the Prophet know when the Hour (Judgment Day) will occur?
questions.answer: No, knowledge of it is only with Allah; it may be that the Hour is near.

questions.text: What will the disbelievers say when their faces are turned about in the Fire?
questions.answer: They will say, 'How we wish we had obeyed Allah and obeyed the Messenger!'

questions.text: Who will the disbelievers blame for their misguidance in Hell?
questions.answer: They will say they obeyed their masters and dignitaries, and they led them astray from the path.

questions.text: What is the 'Trust' (Amanah) mentioned at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The trust of moral responsibility and obedience which the heavens, earth, and mountains refused to carry, but man undertook it.

questions.text: How is man described in his undertaking of the Amanah?
questions.answer: As 'unjust and ignorant' (Zaluman Jahula) for taking on a responsibility he often fails to fulfill.

questions.text: What should believers say to have their deeds amended and sins forgiven?
questions.answer: They should fear Allah and speak a word of appropriate justice (Qawlan Sadida).

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Ahzab'?
questions.answer: The Combined Forces or The Confederates.

questions.text: What is the 'Battle of the Trench' also known as?
questions.answer: The Battle of Al-Ahzab.

questions.text: What is the 'Great Victory' (Fawzan Azima)?
questions.answer: Obeying Allah and His Messenger.

questions.text: What is the 'Illuminating Lamp' (Sirajan Munira)?
questions.answer: A description of the Prophet's role in guiding humanity with the light of revelation.

questions.text: What is the 'Outer Garment' (Jilbab)?
questions.answer: A large sheet or cloak worn by women to cover their bodies in public.

questions.text: What is the 'Appropriate justice' in speech (Qawlan Sadida)?
questions.answer: Speaking the truth, being upright, and avoid deception or vain talk.

questions.text: What is the 'Impurity' (Rijs) Allah wants to remove from the Prophet's household?
questions.answer: The spiritual and moral filth of sin and disobedience.

questions.text: Why is the Prophet's household (Ahl al-Bayt) singled out for purification?
questions.answer: To maintain their dignity and status as role models for the entire community.

questions.text: What is the 'Former times of ignorance' (Jahiliyyat al-Ula)?
questions.answer: The era before Islam characterized by idol worship and lack of modesty.

questions.text: How should the believers treat the Prophet's privacy?
questions.answer: With respect, not entering his house uninvited and not overstaying their welcome.

questions.text: What is the 'Humiliating Punishment' (Adhaban Muhina)?
questions.answer: The punishment for those who hurt Allah and His Messenger.

questions.text: What is the 'Covenant' of the believers mentioned in the context of the battle?
questions.answer: Their pledge to fight and not turn their backs to the enemy.

questions.text: What is the 'Seal' an indication of?
questions.answer: The completion and finality of the chain of divine messengers.

questions.text: What is the 'Trust' (Amanah) in relation to free will?
questions.answer: The choice to obey or disobey Allah, which carries the potential for great reward or punishment.

questions.text: What is the 'Exposed' house (Awrah) claim?
questions.answer: A lie used by the hypocrites to escape military duty.

questions.text: What is the 'Decision of Allah' regarding the Prophet's marriage to Zaynab?
questions.answer: A divine decree that overrode social taboos of the time.

questions.text: What is the 'Witness' (Shahid) role of the Prophet?
questions.answer: Testifying for or against people based on their acceptance of the message.

questions.text: What is the 'Warner' (Nadhir) role?
questions.answer: Cautioning about the consequences of disbelief.

questions.text: What is the 'Glad Tidings' (Bashir) role?
questions.answer: Informing the believers of the rewards of Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'Inviter to Allah' (Da'iyan ilallah) role?
questions.answer: Calling humanity to worship the one true God.

questions.text: What is the 'Blessing' (Salat) from Allah and the angels?
questions.answer: Allah's mercy and the angels' prayers for forgiveness.

questions.text: What is the 'Curse' (La'nah) of Allah?
questions.answer: Being removed from Allah's mercy.

questions.text: What is the 'Sharp tongue' of the hypocrites?
questions.answer: Their criticism and insults toward the believers after a victory.

questions.text: What is the 'Roll of eyes' of the hypocrites?
questions.answer: Their intense fear and cowardice in battle.

questions.text: What is the 'Grief' the Prophet should avoid?
questions.answer: Distress over the plots and rejection of the disbelievers.

questions.text: What is the 'Wisdom' (Hikmah) in the Prophet's homes?
questions.answer: The practical application of the Quran in daily life.

questions.text: What is the 'Noble Recompense' (Karim)?
questions.answer: The honorable rewards in Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'Specified Term' for marriages?
questions.answer: The duration of the legal contract and waiting periods.

questions.text: What is the 'Covenant' of the prophets?
questions.answer: Their agreement to support one another and deliver the same core message.

questions.text: What is the 'Wind' sent against the Ahzab?
questions.answer: A cold, violent wind that uprooted the enemies' tents and equipment.

questions.text: What is the 'Terror' in the hearts of the enemy?
questions.answer: A psychological defeat caused by Allah's intervention.

questions.text: What is the final lesson of Al-Ahzab?
questions.answer: That total trust in Allah and obedience to the Prophet lead to victory, even against overwhelming odds.
